describes: Common insurance policy types, coverage categories, exclusion classes

entries.flood: Flood damage — typically excluded from standard auto and home policies; requires separate flood insurance.
entries.earthquake: Earthquake damage — typically excluded; requires separate earthquake rider.
entries.comprehensive: Comprehensive coverage — covers non-collision damage (theft, vandalism, weather).
entries.collision: Collision coverage — covers damage from collision with another vehicle or object.
entries.liability: Liability coverage — covers damages and injuries to others when policyholder is at fault.
entries.deductible: Deductible — the amount the policyholder pays out-of-pocket before the insurer pays.
entries.premium: Premium — periodic payment to maintain the policy.
entries.exclusion: Exclusion — specific conditions or events the policy does not cover.
entries.rider: Rider — supplemental coverage added to a base policy.
entries.umbrella: Umbrella policy — extra liability coverage beyond underlying policy limits.
